name: Arizona Pecan Chocolate Rum Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the salt into the flour and cut in the lard until the mixture forms a dough.
2. Measure out 1 tablespoon of the beaten eggs into a small bowl and reserve the remaining eggs.
3. Add the water and vinegar to the bowl and stir.
4. Slowly add the liquid to the flour and lard, just until the mixture holds together.
5. Lightly flour a work surface and rolling pin. Roll the dough into an 11-inch circle.
6. Fit it into a 9-inch pie pan and refrigerate if you plan to finish the pie after several hours, or freeze if completing the recipe at this point to prevent shrinkage.
7. Melt the chocolate and butter together in a heavy saucepan and set aside to cool.
8. Stir the corn syrup and sugar into the reserved eggs. Add the melted chocolate and butter, vanilla, rum, and pecans. Mix well and pour into the pie shell.
9. Bake in the preheated oven for 1 hour, or until set. The filling will rise and then fall. Remove from the oven and cool on a rack. Serve slightly warm or at room temperature.
